title: Pros and cons of lease vs buy
description: Practice understanding of costs and benefits of owning versus leasing a vehicle.
topic_path: Pros and cons of lease vs buy

assessment_items.question_content:
When thinking about getting a new car, you might wonder if it's better to lease it or buy it.

**When is *leasing* a better option for you?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: if you drive a lot for work
if you like newest car features
if you like to customize cars
if you need to make payments instead of fully buying a car

assessment_items.question_content:
When thinking about getting a new car, you might wonder if it's better to lease it or buy it.

**When is *leasing* a better option for you?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: if you drive a lot for work
if you don't want to pay for repairs
if you like to customize cars
if you need to make payments instead of fully buying a car

assessment_items.question_content:
When thinking about getting a new car, you might wonder if it's better to lease it or buy it.

**When is *buying* a better option for you?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: if you drive a lot for work
if you like frequently changing a car model
if you like lower monthly payments
If you do not want to worry about repairs

assessment_items.question_content:
Both buying and leasing have their pros and cons.

**Sort the descriptions below as being *pros* of buying vs *pros* of leasing.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
**Buying** a car means it ends up being entirely yours. You can pay for it all at once or get a loan. When you finish paying off the loan, there are no more car payments, and the car is all yours.

**Leasing** a car is similar to renting.  Leasing is only available at dealerships, and only for new cars. You pay every month to use the car, but it’s not yours to keep. Leases typically last two to four years.

###Pros of buying   

- Ownership
- No driving limits
- Customizing

### Pros of leasing

- Lower monthly payments
- Always having a new car
- Fewer repair costs

Benefit | Buying | Leasing
- | :-: | :-:
smaller monthly payment |  | X
unlimited mileage | X | 
ownership | X | 
ability to customize | X |

assessment_items.question_content:
Both buying and leasing have their pros and cons.

**Sort the descriptions below as being *pros* of buying vs *pros* of leasing.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
**Buying** a car means it ends up being entirely yours. You can pay for it all at once or get a loan. When you finish paying off the loan, there are no more car payments, and the car is all yours. 

**Leasing** a car is similar to renting.  Leasing is only available at dealerships, and only for new cars. You pay every month to use the car, but it’s not yours to keep. Leases typically last two to four years.

###Pros of buying   

- Ownership
- No mileage limits
- Customizing is allowed

### Pros of leasing

- Drive a new car more often
- No need to worry about vehicle value
- Fewer repair costs

Benefit | Buying | Leasing
- | :-: | :-:
little to no repair costs |  | X
unlimited mileage | X | 
not worrying about trade-in value |  | X
ability to customize | X |

assessment_items.question_content:
Both buying and leasing have their pros and cons.

**Sort the descriptions below as being *cons* of buying vs *cons* of leasing.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
**Buying** a car means it ends up being entirely yours. You can pay for it all at once or get a loan. When you finish paying off the loan, there are no more car payments, and the car is all yours. 

**Leasing** a car is similar to renting.  Leasing is only available at dealerships, and only for new cars. You pay every month to use the car, but it’s not yours to keep. Leases typically last two to four years.

###Cons of buying   

- Monthly payments are usually high
- Knowing the vehicle will probably lose value
- Ownership will probably last longer than the warranty

### Cons of leasing

- There is a limit on how many miles you can drive
- You will always have a monthly car payment
- Not allowed to customize the vehicle

Con | Buying | Leasing
- | :-: | :-:
limits on mileage |  | X
no customizing |  | X
higher monthly payments |X| 
always having a payment |  | X

assessment_items.question_content:
You have been leasing a car for two years and your lease is nearing its end. 

**What happens to the car at the end of the lease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: you own it
you return it to the dealership
you must sell it

assessment_items.question_content:
You have been leasing a car for two years and your lease is nearing its end. 

**How much do you owe at the end of the lease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: zero dollars
remaining balance
trade-in value

assessment_items.question_content:
You lease a new car to drive back and forth to work. A week later it starts making grinding noise.

**Who pays for the repairs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the vehicle manufacturer
you do
insurance

assessment_items.question_content:
You have financed and been making payments on your car for four years.  Your loan term is near its end. 

**What happens to the car once you make the last payment?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: you own it
you return it to the dealership
you must sell it

assessment_items.question_content:
You've finished paying off your financed car.

**Did you pay more, less, or the same compared to leasing?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: more
less
the same

assessment_items.question_content:
You drive a lot and have chosen to finance a car.

**At the end of your financing term, what happens with extra miles?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: you pay extra for going over a limit
nothing, there are no limits on miles
it depends on the lender
